rationale: The citing paper uses Khan and Dhar (2010) in a specific methodological role, not just as background. In Experiment 3, the authors explicitly cite it when they frame the same product as hedonic versus utilitarian to control for product-level confounds. This design choice is important for their mechanism test, because it helps isolate framing-based self-signaling effects on donation interest.
relation_description: Savary et al. apply Khan and Dhar's hedonic-versus-utilitarian framing approach to construct Experiment 3 with an identical product framed differently. They use this borrowed setup to test their donation mechanism while ruling out differences across distinct reference products.
